pattern: A dynamic program over target distances chooses between overshooting with a full run of accelerations then reversing, or stopping short and reversing twice, to find the fewest instructions.
hints: After k accelerations from a standstill the car has moved 2^k - 1 and reached speed 2^k, so think in terms of how many full doublings to use before reversing.
Either accelerate exactly enough to land on or past the target then reverse to cover the remainder, or accelerate to just short of it, reverse, back up a little, and reverse again.

Let dp[t] be the shortest length to reach t. For each t pick the smallest k with 2^k - 1 >= t: if it equals t use k; otherwise combine an overshoot plus a turn, and combine stopping short, a turn, and an inner back-up, taking the minimum.

edge_inputs.description: first example, reachable with a clean run of accelerations
second example, requires an overshoot then reverse
just past a clean run, needs back-up moves
value one below a power of two boundary

stress.description: assorted targets across the whole range
stress.note: the dp is linear in target so even the maximum is fast
